instructions: For questions 29 to 40, write your answers in this booklet. The number of marks available is shown in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question.

parts.context: The diagram shows half a seed. Part Q protects the baby plant.
parts.question: State another function of part Q.

parts.context: Melvin observed stages in the life of fish T and fish W as shown. Fish T: egg → young (stage 1, with stored food) → young (stage 2) → adult. Fish W: young → adult (gives birth to young).

subparts.question: Based on Melvin's observation, state a characteristic of the young of fish T that helps it to survive in stage 1.
State a characteristic of the young of fish W which gives it an advantage to survive compared to the young of fish T at stage 1. Explain your answer.

subparts.context: Fish T lays its eggs inside a cave as shown. It is known to protect its eggs.
subparts.question: Explain how laying eggs in a cave helps fish T to protect its eggs.

questions.context: Bacteria M can cause illness when it is present in large numbers. Rahim conducted an experiment to find out how quickly bacteria M can reproduce when it is kept at different temperatures. Results: 30 °C: at start 1, 24 hrs later 120; 35 °C: 1, 250; 40 °C: 1, 130; 45 °C: 1, 60.

subparts.question: Describe how the amount of bacteria M changes with temperature.

subparts.context: Bacteria M can be found on raw food. The human body temperature is 37 °C.
subparts.question: Based on the information given, suggest why bacteria M will reproduce quickly in the human intestines when raw food is eaten.

parts.context: Rahim carried out a second experiment to find out if bacteria M can be killed at high temperatures. He heated the same amount of bacteria at different temperatures for 15 seconds. His results are shown above (graph showing amount of bacteria M left after 15 seconds vs temperature 80, 90, 100 °C - bacteria amount decreases as temperature increases). He cooked some raw food to 100 °C. The cooked food was left at 30 °C for several hours.
parts.question: Based on the results of Rahim's two experiments, explain why the cooked food may no longer be safe to eat.

questions.context: A farmer observed a food relationship on his farm: plant E → insect B. The farmer learnt that another insect, L, is a predator of insect B. Insect L also likes to feed on pollen. Using this information, he grew plant E between rows of plant D as shown.
questions.question: Based on the information above, explain how this farming method allows the farmer to grow plant E without pesticides.

parts.context: The diagram shows cells during a process H in the reproduction of an animal. Part C controls all the activities of the cell.
parts.question: Name part C.

parts.context: Process H also occurs in plant P. The diagram shows how a fruit is formed from the flower of plant P. The fruit of plant P has been cut open. flower → Process G → Process H → fruit (with seed J).

subparts.question: State the part of the flower that J developed from.

parts.context: In a population of plant K, each plant has only red flowers or only white flowers.
parts.question: A farmer collected seeds from plant K with red flowers to grow new plants. Explain why the new plants will have only red flowers.

questions.context: A scientist carried out an experiment to measure the volume of blood supplied per minute to different parts of the human body during two activities: resting and running. Graph shows: legs - resting low, running very high; small intestine - resting moderate, running very low.

parts.question: Describe how oxygen in the environment reaches the legs.
Using the graph above, explain how running after a meal affects the absorption of digested food in the small intestine.

parts.context: Four parts of a plant are shown below (stem, leaves, roots, flowers).
parts.question: Draw arrows in the diagram below to show how water is transported in a plant.

subparts.question: Describe the process of photosynthesis in green plants.

subparts.context: Insect R eats its way into a tree trunk. This affects the growth of the roots of the tree.
subparts.question: Explain why.

parts.context: Plant T grows in a desert. It is able to store air in the leaves. Carl observed the cells of a leaf of plant T during the day (Day 45 °C: tiny opening closed) and at night (Night 9 °C: tiny opening open).
parts.question: Based on Carl's observations, explain how plant T is adapted for photosynthesis in a desert.

parts.context: A metal lid was fitted tightly onto an empty glass jar. Mary placed the lid in hot water.
parts.question: After a while, she found it easier to remove the lid. Explain why.

parts.context: A thick-walled glass placed in boiling water.
parts.question: When Mary took a cold thick-walled glass from a refrigerator and placed it in boiling water as shown above, the glass cracked. Explain why.
State a difference between evaporation and boiling.

parts.context: Fatimah fried some bananas and put two pieces each in box R (closed lid, no holes) and box S (lid with holes). She observed that the bananas in box R became slightly wet after some time, but not those in box S.
parts.question: Explain why the bananas in box R became wet.

questions.context: Dingwei wanted to investigate how the number of batteries affects the number of paper clips attracted by a rod (electromagnet) using the set-up shown. He placed the rod 1 cm from some paper clips and counted the number of paper clips that were attracted to it. Results: 3 batteries - 6 clips; 4 batteries - 6 clips.

parts.question: Name one suitable material for the rod.
Suggest two reasons why the number of paper clips attracted did not increase when the number of batteries was increased from three to four.

questions.context: Muthu performed an experiment on two different types of springs, P and Q, of the same length. He measured the extension of the spring after adding a mass. Results: Mass 100 g → P: 2.9 cm, Q: 1.9 cm; 200 g → 6.1, 4.0; 300 g → 9.0, 5.9; 400 g → 12.1, 8.1.

parts.question: Based on his results, state the relationship between the mass and extension of spring P.
Explain the purpose of the pin in the set-up.

parts.context: Muthu used spring P to make a dish rack as shown in Diagram 2 (positions A, B, C on the stretched spring with a metal disc).
parts.question: When Muthu removed a few plates quickly from the top, the metal disc moved up continuously from A to C. Name the main forms of energy of the metal disc at B.

parts.context: Next, Muthu changed all the spring P to spring Q. He put back all the plates on the metal disc.
parts.question: Based on the results in Table 1, describe one difference Muthu will observe of the new dish rack with spring Q when he removes a plate from the top. Explain your answer.

questions.context: John wanted to compare how fast a wooden block can move on different planks using the set-up below. He released the block, measured the distance moved by the block down the plank and the time taken.

parts.context: A shorter plank made of the same material as R was placed at the same height, h. Will the block take a shorter, longer or the same time to reach the bottom of the plank?
parts.question: Explain your answer in terms of energy changes.

parts.context: John's results for plank R (graph of distance vs time, curve up to 80 cm by 2 s) shown. John repeated the experiment with a wooden plank of the same length. The wooden block moved down the plank more slowly.

subparts.question: State which graph, J, K, L or M shows the correct results for the wooden plank. (J highest curve, L next, K, M lowest)
Besides the height, h and the length of the plank, state two other variables that John must keep constant for the comparison.

questions.context: Poh Lee wants to remove water from the ground using a water pump when it rains heavily. He uses the set-up: metal rods, water pump connected via rubber to metal container, ground level with metal sheet over wooden block, water enters container through hole at G.

parts.question: State a property of rubber that allows the set-up to work properly.
Describe and explain how the water pump is turned on when it rains heavily.
Without using different apparatus, suggest one way to turn on the water pump when it rains less heavily. Explain your answer.
